items.headline: City updates affordable housing protections if subsidies end or foreclosure occurs
items.summary: San José is modifying its policies to ensure affordable housing units remain affordable even if project subsidies expire or properties go into foreclosure. This addresses a gap where affordable units could convert to market-rate housing when funding agreements end.

items.headline: City-wide storm sewer system master plan moves forward with consultant
items.summary: The City Council approved an amendment to hire Wood Rodgers, Inc. to develop a comprehensive master plan for San José's storm sewer infrastructure. This planning work will guide major investments in the city's aging stormwater system.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development advances with four new homes
items.summary: Four new single-family homes permitted along Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development. This represents a significant residential expansion in the area with homes ranging from $487k to $573k.

items.headline: Sherly Place residential development gets three new homes
items.summary: Three new single-family homes permitted at Sherly Place development with prices ranging from $496k to $570k. Combined with the Comm Hill permits, this signals active new housing construction in San José.

items.headline: South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station project cost increase approved
items.summary: The Council increased the construction contract contingency for the South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 Bypass Project following a rebid. The project is part of the region's water recycling and infrastructure improvements.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's planned capital projects for the next fiscal year. This is where major infrastructure investments, development projects, and city improvements are discussed before formal approval.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which governs rules affecting renters in Sunnyvale. The specific changes weren't detailed in the agenda, but this could affect housing protections, eviction processes, or tenant rights in the city.
